metadata.title: What Hollow Knight's First Game Doc Gets Right

metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Seth Brown — Humanitarians AI Fellow. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Hollow Knight's world tells its story through aftermath — the Crossroads are already infected when you arrive. No narrator explains what happened; the emptiness itself functions as the character. A $56,000 AUD stretch goal committed the team to Hornet as a second playable character. The first game doc shows storytelling carried by environment and funding milestones, not exposition.

artifactLines: Hollow Knight's world tells its story through aftermath — the Crossroads are already infected when you arrive.
No narrator explains what happened; the emptiness itself functions as the character.
A $56,000 AUD stretch goal committed the team to Hornet as a second playable character.
The first game doc shows storytelling carried by environment and funding milestones, not exposition.

beats.narration_text: The line 'the emptiness is the character' captures exactly what I want to learn from Hollow Knight's first game doc. Help me (1) break down how the infected Crossroads tells its story as aftermath, with no narrator explaining what happened, (2) trace how a Kickstarter stretch goal like Hornet at $56,000 AUD transferred from wishlist to committed design, and (3) draft the same kind of one-line environmental-storytelling brief for a small area in a game idea of my own.
